name: Socca
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the dried rosemary or thyme with olive oil and gently heat over very low heat for about 2 minutes.
2. Allow the mixture to cool for 2-3 minutes. If using rosemary, strain the oil through a fine mesh strainer into a separate bowl; set aside (no need to strain if using thyme).
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine the chickpea flour and salt with the pepper.
4. Gradually add warm water to the dry ingredients and whisk until a smooth batter forms.
5. Add the herb-infused olive oil to the batter and whisk again until well combined.
6. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the batter rest for 20 minutes.
7. Heat a small skillet or crepe pan over medium heat and lightly coat with oil.
8. Pour about 1/4 cup of batter into the center of the hot pan, tilting the pan to spread it evenly into a roughly 6-inch circle.
9. Cook until the bottom is golden brown for approximately 30 seconds, then use a spatula to flip the crepe and cook for an additional 10-15 seconds on the other side.
10. Remove the cooked crepe from the pan and place it on a plate, keeping it covered.
11. Repeat steps 7-10 until all the batter is used, which should yield about 12 crepes.
12. Serve the crepes as-is or roll them into cylinders for dipping, or fill with your favorite spreads (cheese spreads, tapenade, hummus, etc.).
